steps.title: Test the proxy link
steps.content: The hypothesis is that Ag-NP bioaccumulation in P. pruinosus could be used as a proxy for Ag-NP bioaccumulation in X. laevis. For that proxy role to work, measurements in the invertebrate must reliably predict measurements in the frog.

steps.title: Find the broken prediction
steps.content: A only says P. pruinosus accumulates other nanoparticles, which does not address whether Ag-NP levels predict frog levels. B concerns fewer negative effects, not predictive accuracy. D says individuals within each species do not vary much, which would make measurements more consistent. The weakening finding is the one saying P. pruinosus Ag-NP concentrations are not predictive of X. laevis concentrations in comparable environments. The answer is C.
